raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Establishing Civic and Voter Empowerment Coordinators will provide California Universities and Colleges with tools to increase civic engagement among young voters.",
    "Data shows that when college campuses develop an action plan for voter engagement, it results in strong voter turnout.",
    "UCLA reported a 537% increase in in-person student voters during the 2018 midterm election as a result of their participation in the ALL IN Campus Democracy Challenge.",
    "AB 963 aims to designate a Civic and Voter Empowerment Coordinator on each campus to nurture an engaged and informed electorate.",
    "AB 963 seeks to empower a new generation of voters, particularly among immigrant, low-income, and youth of color.",
    "Education is identified as the biggest barrier to voting among California students.",
    "Only 8.2% of registered California students voted in 2014.",
    "The Student Civic and Voter Empowerment Act is designed to decrease barriers that students face when trying to become civically engaged and educated.",
    "Establishing resources for civic engagement on college campuses can positively impact the lives of 3.1 million students in California.",
    "Incorporating voting into the college process is essential for developing voting habits among the youth.",
    "Empowering civic engagement and strengthening voter rights can mitigate the negative impacts of bad-acting politicians and student loan policies."
  ]
}
```
